{{Quote|The nature-loving Ithorians know a thing or two about what makes a great salad. This is our take on the traditional covado salad—mixed Ithorian greens garnished with starfruit and hard-boiled ooglata eggs.|Dexter Jettster, menu for Dex's Diner|<ref name="Dex" />}}
'''Ithorian starfruit''' was a variety of [[Starfruit/Legends|starfruit]] that was [[Foodstuff|edible]] by [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorians]] and many other [[Species/Legends|species]]. It could be [[Diet/Legends|eaten]] plain or used in other dishes. Ithorian starfruit was served around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], including within the [[Restaurant/Legends|eatery]] [[Dex's Diner/Legends|Dex's Diner]] on the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

==Description==
Ithorian starfruit, a variety of [[Starfruit/Legends|starfruit]], was a type of [[Fruit/Legends|fruit]]<ref name="Dex" /> whose peel came off easily when the fruit was overripe.<ref name="Revenge of the Sith novel" /> The [[Foodstuff/Legends|fruit]] could be [[Diet/Legends|consumed]] by a variety of [[Species/Legends|species]], including [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]], [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]], [[Cerean/Legends|Cereans]], [[Duros/Legends|Duros]], [[Herglic/Legends|Herglics]], [[Human/Legends|Humans]], [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorians]], [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustans]], [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'leks]], [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]], and [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltrons]]. Ithorian starfruit could be blended into a light [[starfruit creme liqueur]].<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> It could also be used as a garnish on [[Salad/Legends|salads]] such as on the [[herd ship's bounty salad]], a variant of the [[covado salad]].<ref name="Dex" />

==History==
[[Dexter Jettster/Legends|Dexter Jettster]], the owner of the [[Restaurant/Legends|eatery]] [[Dex's Diner/Legends|Dex's Diner]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], served the herd ship's bounty salad in his restaurant, which included Ithorian starfruit as a garnish. In his menu, he complimented the Ithorians on their salad skills.<ref name="Dex" /> In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="Coruscant Date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]] to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].</ref> during the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] dodged an explosion that he believed would have peeled back the [[Armor/Legends|armor]] on his [[Obi-Wan Kenobi's Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|starfighter]] like an overripe Ithorian starfruit.<ref name="Revenge of the Sith novel" /> When the luxury [[Hovertrain/Legends|repulsortrain]] ''[[Gem of Alderaan]]'' launched<ref name="Gem of Alderaan" /> sometime around [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]]<ref name="GoA Date">"[[Echoes of the Jedi]]," the fourth installment of the ''[[Dawn of Defiance]]'' campaign, states that the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vhiin Thorla]] traveled to the [[Almas Academy]] two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] was issued, an event that is dated to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. "[[The Gem of Alderaan]]" is said to take place at least six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] afterward, placing the launch at some point around [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]].</ref> on the planet [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], starfruit creme liqueur was served in the dining car.<ref name="Gem of Alderaan">{{DoD|The Gem of Alderaan}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Starfruit.png|thumb|right|63px|Starfruit in ''Tiny Death Star'']]
Ithorian starfruit was first mentioned in [[Matthew Stover]]'s [[2005]] [[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|novelization]] of the [[prequel trilogy]] film of the same year, [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']].<ref name="Revenge of the Sith novel" /> In the non-[[canon]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Tiny Death Star]]'', released in [[2013]],<ref name="TIHOM2">{{WebCite|url=http://mixnmojo.com/news/Tiny-Death-Star-Available-For-iOS-in-Australia|text=Tiny Death Star Available For iOS in Australia|work=[http://mixnmojo.com The International House of Mojo]|archivedate=20140702141800}}</ref> Ithorian starfruit made its first appearance and received a visual depiction for the first time, depicted as a small [[Color/Legends|pink]] fruit. It was served in the "Ithorian Food" level of [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star I]], cost 350 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], and took thirty-five minutes to prepare.<ref name="Tiny Death Star">''[[Star Wars: Tiny Death Star]]''</ref>
